title: Excoecaria loureiroana
intro: Excoecaria loureiroana, commonly known as the Yellow Mangrove or Mangrove Stinging Tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Euphorbiaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to treat various ailments such as wound healing, fever reduction, and digestive issues. Excoecaria loureiroana contains bioactive constituents like flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or a tincture, and is commonly used to make a paste or an ointment for topical application.
taxonomy: Excoecaria loureiroana is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Euphorbiaceae. It belongs to the genus Excoecaria and is native to Southeast Asia. Gagnep. within the family Euphorbiaceae, order Malpighiales, and class Magnoliopsida. It is also known for its toxic sap and has been used in traditional medicine in some regions.

The plant's leaves and bark are traditionally used in folk medicine to treat a range of ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions.
